name: Highway Employees
description_short: Highway Benefits is an HR platform that helps employers offer tax-free student loan repayment and tuition assistance benefits to attract and retain talent.
description_long:
Highway Benefits is a benefits administration platform designed for employers seeking to improve employee financial wellness through educational assistance programs. The company positions itself as a solution for HR teams looking to differentiate their compensation packages in a competitive talent market, with particular emphasis on student debt management as a recruitment and retention tool.

The platform offers three primary services: employer student loan repayment (SLR) programs that provide tax-free contributions toward employee student loans, tuition assistance and reimbursement (TA/TR) benefits for ongoing education, and student loan retirement plan matching features that verify qualified student loan payments for 401(k), 403(b), SIMPLE IRA, and 457(b) plans. Highway handles the administrative backend, automation, and employee education components, allowing HR teams to launch and manage these programs with minimal ongoing effort.

Highway distinguishes itself through its focus on the intersection of financial wellness and talent management. The company emphasizes that student loan repayment benefits are permanently tax-free for both employers and employees, and cites statistics showing 41% of recent graduates rank this benefit in their top three priorities and 86% of employees would commit to staying five years if employers helped pay student loans. The platform serves not just direct employers but also works with brokers to reach clients, and provides educational resources for employees themselves.

However, Highway is fundamentally a B2B benefits platform for employers, not a direct consumer financial services product. It requires employer adoption and enrollment, making it inaccessible to self-employed individuals or employees at companies that don't offer the program. The service is also most valuable to employees already burdened with student debt, and provides no assistance to those without educational loans or for non-student-related financial emergencies.
services: Employer student loan repayment (SLR) program administration
Tuition assistance and reimbursement (TA/TR) benefit management
Student loan retirement plan matching (QSLP) verification for 401(k), 403(b), SIMPLE IRA, 457(b)
Free benefit plan creation and configuration tool
Employee education hub with student loan resources and tools
Case study and program performance reporting
Broker partnership and client education support
Demo and consultation services for plan design
pros: Tax-free benefit structure for both employers and employees on student loan repayments
Automates compliance and administration, allowing HR teams to focus on other functions
Integrates with existing 401(k), 403(b), SIMPLE IRA, and 457(b) retirement plans for matching
Provides free plan creation tool to set eligibility and contribution amounts before commitment
Includes educational content hub and resources to help employees navigate student loans
Addresses demonstrated employee demand (41% of recent grads prioritize this benefit)
Reduces documented employee turnover (86% would stay 5+ years for this benefit)
cons: Requires employer adoption—no direct access for individual consumers without employer enrollment
Limited utility for employees without student loan debt or those seeking other types of financial assistance
No emergency funding or short-term cash solutions for immediate financial needs
Pricing and specific program limits not disclosed on website; requires demo request
Effectiveness depends entirely on employer contribution amounts and eligibility policies set by each company
best_for: Mid-to-large employers competing for talent in competitive labor markets
HR leaders implementing comprehensive financial wellness programs
Employees at companies offering Highway with significant student loan balances
Professional services and technology companies targeting recent college graduates
diagnosis: Highway Benefits is a B2B employer benefits platform, not a consumer lending or financial assistance company. It is best for employers seeking to attract and retain talent through student debt assistance, but is only accessible to employees whose companies have adopted the program. This service addresses talent management and employee financial wellness, not direct consumer credit or emergency funding needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
